caption: Describe the theory of operation.

messages_sent_to_router.user_text:
Drawing type: wiring diagram

No OCR labels were extracted; rely on the image.

response:
## 1. What this appears to be
This appears to be a page from a technical manual, specifically detailing configuration notes for using device connectors with 3SK1 safety relays.

## 2. Main visible components
The main visible components are:
- Text blocks with configuration notes
- A notice about the use of 3SK1 Advanced basic units
- A warning about supply voltage
- Notes about power supply configurations

## 3. Plain-English theory of operation
The document provides guidelines for configuring device connectors with 3SK1 safety relays:
- Only one 3SK1 Advanced basic unit is permitted per system.
- The supply voltage must be 24 V DC and only fed into the basic unit.
- If expansion units are controlled via 3ZY12 device connectors, additional control supply voltage at one of the output expansions will cause a system fault.
- The 3SK1230 power supply must be located on the extreme left if used.
- A 24 V DC supply voltage from an external power supply unit can only be connected to the terminals on the basic unit.

## 4. What must be true for it to work
For the system to work:
- Only one 3SK1 Advanced basic unit per system.
- Supply voltage must be 24 V DC into the basic unit.
- If using a 3SK1230 power supply, it must be on the extreme left.
- No additional 24 V DC connections are permitted if a 3SK1230 power supply is used.

## 5. What would stop it from working
The system would not work if:
- More than one 3SK1 Advanced basic unit is used.
- Supply voltage is not 24 V DC or not fed into the basic unit.
- Additional control supply voltage is fed at one of the output expansions when using 3ZY12 device connectors.
- A 3SK1230 power supply is not on the extreme left or if an additional 24 V DC connection is made when it's used.

## 6. Unclear or unreadable items
Nothing major appears to be unreadable in the provided image.
